summary: Impersonation of Anthropic's claude-code wrapper republished under unrelated @bonsai-ai scope
details: Package is published as @bonsai-ai/claude-code but its metadata and documentation impersonate Anthropic's official @anthropic-ai/claude-code: package.json declares `author: "Anthropic <support@anthropic.com>"` and `homepage: github.com/anthropics/claude-code`, the README instructs users to `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`, and the placeholder stub at bin/claude.exe even references `node node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-code/install.cjs`. The postinstall script (install.cjs) resolves a platform-specific sibling under the same @bonsai-ai scope via optionalDependencies, copies its `claude` binary over bin/claude.exe, and chmods it 0o755 — making the unrelated publisher's binary the user's system-wide `claude` CLI. The native binary itself ships from a separate @bonsai-ai-controlled package and cannot be inspected from this tarball. The combination of (a) deliberate publisher impersonation in metadata and README, (b) a name collision with a widely-used official package, and (c) a postinstall step that installs an opaque binary from the unrelated scope as a trusted tool is a typosquat-with-dropper shape: installers who mistype the scope or follow a poisoned suggestion will run attacker-scoped code believing it to be Anthropic's official Claude Code CLI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
